Checks syntax of reStructuredText and code blocks nested within it.

See the full documentation at [read-the-docs]

```{contents}
```

## Installation

From pip

```shell
$ pip install rstcheck
```

To use pyproject.toml for configuration:

```
$ pip install rstcheck[toml]
```

To add sphinx support:

```
$ pip install rstcheck[sphinx]
```

## Supported languages in code blocks

- Bash
- Doctest
- C (C99)
- C++ (C++11)
- JSON
- XML
- Python
- reStructuredText

## Examples

% rstcheck: ignore-languages=cpp,python,rst

With bad Python syntax:

```rst
====
Test
====

.. code:: python

    print(
```

```text
$ rstcheck bad_python.rst
bad_python.rst:7: (ERROR/3) (python) unexpected EOF while parsing
```

With bad C++ syntax:

```rst
====
Test
====

.. code:: cpp

    int main()
    {
        return x;
    }
```

```text
$ rstcheck bad_cpp.rst
bad_cpp.rst:9: (ERROR/3) (cpp) error: 'x' was not declared in this scope
```

With bad syntax in the reStructuredText document itself:

```rst
====
Test
===
```

```text
$ rstcheck bad_rst.rst
bad_rst.rst:1: (SEVERE/4) Title overline & underline mismatch.
```

modules.name: rstcheck.__version__
documentation: don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: rstcheck.__main__
documentation:
Executes CLI application's `main` function.

This module acts as the entry point for the application. When executed as the
main script, it calls the `main` function from the ._cli submodule to start the
application.

functions.name: rstcheck._cli.setup_logger
documentation:
Set up logging.

```
param loglevel

Level to log at.

raises TypeError

On invalid logging levels.
```
